First Status Report Of Administrator With Will Annexed Of The Estate Of Leah Joyce Forsmann And Petition For Termination Of Further Proceedings And Discharge Of Administator; Verification
Off Calendar Dec-23-2013 Granted Per Written Order

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of Petition For Final Distribution Or Report On Status Of Administration
Off Calendar Sep-24-2013 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Oct-05-2010 Granted Per Written Order

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Mar-23-2010 Continued To Oct-05-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Freedman for Sylvia Forsman. John Boessenecker for Robin Forsman.

Counsel expressed concern about the settlement not identifying the causes of action being settled, stated that there was a trial date set for September 20, 2010 for the remaining Defendant Hospice by The Bay, that there was a binding arbitration as to who got what and what for, that there was no determination as to whether any of this settlement is for the Elder Abuse claim and that the settlement proceeds would be interpleaded. Mr. Boessenecker stated that he was asked by Robin and Stephen to represent them in the arbitration proceeding. The matter was continued to October 5, 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on the Petition for Probate and the will contest.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Mar-09-2010 Continued To Mar-23-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Dec-01-2009 Continued To Mar-09-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Apr-01-2009 Continued To Dec-01-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Boessenecker for Sylvia Forsmann.

Mr. Boessenecker informed the Court that Mr. Freeman was not present due to a conflict. In addition, he reported that the personal injury litigation case was set for trial in November in Butte or Shasta county, that he was not sure if there were any assets in the estate and requested a continuance. The Court informed Counsel that the summons on the will contest was not served. The matter was continued to December 1, 2009 for status on the petition and for status on the wrongful death action.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: T. Jackson.
